user_query:
I’m updating a Grade 2 unit and want to ground our children’s book choices in data and a colleague’s (networking professional) recommendations. Please do the following using only the provided local files and save all outputs under the paths I specify:

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- input/books_catalog.csv — a catalog of children’s books with fields: title, author, grade_band, theme_tags (semicolon-delimited), pages
- input/recommendations_from_network.jsonl — one JSON object per line with fields: title, reason, topic
- input/allowed_grade_bands.json — allowed grade bands
- input/syllabus.md — my current unit syllabus with a marked placeholder section

Deliverables and requirements:
1) Curate grounded recommendations by cross-referencing:
   - Match recommendation titles in input/recommendations_from_network.jsonl to titles in input/books_catalog.csv (case-insensitive exact match on the full title). Ignore recommended titles that do not exist in the catalog.
   - For each matched book, create a record with fields: title, author, grade_band, themes (array derived from theme_tags, split on ";", trimmed), pages (integer), recommendation_reason (from the JSONL reason), source set to "network_pro".
   - Sort records by grade_band (ascending using the allowed order from input/allowed_grade_bands.json) then by title (A–Z).
   - Write the curated list to output/recommendations_curated.json as an array of objects with exactly those fields.

2) Compute statistics and aggregates over the curated set and save to output/recommendation_stats.json with this structure:
   {
     "total": <integer>,
     "by_grade_band": {"K-2": <integer>, "3-5": <integer>, ... only bands present},
     "top_themes": [ {"theme": "<string>", "count": <integer>} ],  // top 5 by frequency across all curated books, tie-break alphabetically
     "pages_mean": <number>,
     "pages_median": <number>
   }
   - Compute pages_mean and pages_median from the curated pages field.
   - Derive top_themes by exploding the themes arrays and counting occurrences; list at most 5.

3) Edit the existing syllabus by filling the marked section only and save to output/syllabus_updated.md:
   - In input/syllabus.md, replace the content strictly between the markers:
     <!-- BEGIN:RECOMMENDATIONS --> and <!-- END:RECOMMENDATIONS -->
   - Insert, in this order:
     a) A single paragraph (3–5 sentences) that summarizes the grade band distribution and the top themes using values from output/recommendation_stats.json.
     b) A bulleted list of all curated books in the format: "Title — Author (Grade band) — Themes: theme1, theme2, ..." (themes comma-separated in the order they appear for each book).
   - Do not change any content outside the markers.

4) Provide and run a validation script, and save a report:
   - Implement a small validation script at scripts/validate (you may choose an extension like .py or .js) that performs these checks:
     • Schema check: Every object in output/recommendations_curated.json has all required fields (title, author, grade_band, themes[array], pages[int], recommendation_reason[string], source["network_pro"]).
     • Cross-reference: Every curated title exists in input/books_catalog.csv (case-insensitive), and every curated grade_band is a member of input/allowed_grade_bands.json's allowed list.
     • Count consistency: The number of curated records equals the number of recommended titles that matched the catalog.
     • Syllabus linkage: Every curated title appears at least once in output/syllabus_updated.md.
   - Run the script and save a plain-text report with each check labeled PASS/FAIL to output/validation_report.txt. The task is complete only if all checks pass.

Notes:
- Treat theme_tags in the catalog as semicolon-delimited; trim whitespace when creating the themes array.
- Do not use the Internet; rely entirely on the provided inputs.
- Ensure all paths match exactly as specified.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
